sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presented with a history of a right knee injury sustained one week ago while engaging in trampolining at a trampoline park.
He reports hearing a snap and instant pain in the right knee, which he describes as pain on the outside of the knee.
He has been managing his pain with Tylenol but reports it has not provided significant relief.
He denies any other symptoms.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension, for which he is currently on lisinopril 20mg.
He also has diabetes, for which he is taking metformin 500mg.
He reports inconsistent exercise and a diet that could be improved.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications:
Lisinopril 20mg [INDICATION: hypertension]
Metformin 500mg [INDICATION: diabetes]
Objective:
Vital signs:
Blood pressure: 180/80
Pulse rate: NOT MENTIONED
Oxygen saturation: NOT MENTIONED
Temperature: NOT MENTIONED
Physical exam: The patient's blood pressure is elevated at 180/80.
The heart rate is regular and rhythmic.
No murmur is appreciated.
Lungs are clear bilaterally.
Examination of the right knee reveals ecchymosis and edema along the lateral aspect, but no effusion.
Range of motion is present but limited by pain.
Test Results:
Right knee x-ray: No evidence of fracture or bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient presents with a lateral ligament strain of the right knee and hypertension that is not well-controlled, as well as poorly managed diabetes.
Problem list: Right knee pain; hypertension; diabetes.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg daily for pain and swelling of the right knee.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to help strengthen the muscles around the area and prevent further injury.
I will continue the patient's lisinopril at 20mg and order an echocardiogram to assess cardiac function.
I also ordered an A1c and lipid panel to evaluate the patient's diabetes management.
Follow-up: The patient will return for a follow-up appointment to discuss the results of the imaging and laboratory tests.
